key_lessons: Keep business behavior behind thin entry points and explicit service boundaries.
Treat data operations as bulk-capable unless proven otherwise.
Use idempotency for external submissions, generated artifacts, file processing, email replies, and retries.
Use dynamic dependencies only for optional behavior with diagnostics.
Separate before-save normalization from after-save side effects.
Use narrow recursion guards instead of global skip flags.
Define source-of-truth rules for cross-object synchronization.
Handle delayed file and related-record side effects.
Assert behavior and durable async outcomes, not coverage-only execution.
Test blanks, missing configuration, failed callouts, duplicate attempts, and unchanged-record paths.
Avoid org-state dependencies and private IDs.
Treat file-heavy tests and deploy-bound branch coverage as special cases.
Read component metadata before editing behavior.
Choose LDS/UI API or Apex intentionally by data and policy needs.
Move template expressions into getters.
Refresh wire and record state explicitly after mutations.
Treat Salesforce mobile as a distinct runtime.
Prove overlay, camera, preview, handoff, and cleanup states in the target runtime.
Avoid blur-dependent selection in mobile menus.
Use durable handoff strategies for mobile-generated files and PDFs.
Treat PDFs as output contracts.
Preserve stable Visualforce PDF layout primitives.
Review escape=false and rich text as a sanitization risk.
Test launchers in the same Lightning or mobile context users use.
Model files through ContentVersion, ContentDocument, and ContentDocumentLink.
Use latest-version logic for file state and rollups.
Refresh file UI and parent records after upload, delete, retype, or generated output.
Authorize portal and public file access server-side.
Separate actor context, target record, body text, mention ranges, and fallback behavior.
Use durable identifiers for email threading and idempotency.
Do not publish raw message bodies or post bodies.
Test permissions and service-user behavior.
Write compound address leaf fields instead of root fields.
Keep country and state code values consistent.
Use structured fields as source of truth.
Treat geocode and map features as optional derived behavior.
A deployed component is not necessarily visible.
Quick action visibility requires checking metadata, layout, Dynamic Actions, overflow, form factor, activation, and permissions.
Avoid broad profile, FlexiPage, layout, report, dashboard, and static resource churn.
Prefer targeted permission sets where project conventions allow.
Validate the smallest deployable set first.
Use explicit test strategy and branch coverage for touched Apex.
Separate destructive deployments from feature deploys.
Verify installed sf command shape and Windows quoting before scripting.
Classify failure layer before fixing.
Use visible instrumentation for mobile and webview issues when logs are insufficient.
Treat CLI probes as evidence, not absolute truth.
Summarize logs into symptom, root cause, fix, and validation.

topics_found.topic: Public safety and repo hygiene patterns
key_lessons: Private references are evidence, not publishable text.
Rewrite lessons in neutral Salesforce terms.
Never publish raw logs, screenshots, generated documents, email bodies, Chatter bodies, IDs, or credentials.
Draft and scan private-derived lessons before moving them into repo knowledge files.

p0_improvements.title: Create communication automation coverage
p0_improvements.rationale: The local references repeatedly cover Chatter, email, activities, mentions, actor context, idempotency, and logging, while the current repo has no dedicated communication automation topic.

p0_improvements.title: Create destructive deployment safety guidance
p0_improvements.rationale: Deletion metadata needs a separate review and validation workflow before any public prompt encourages destructive changes.

p0_improvements.title: Expand file, photo, and mobile lifecycle guidance
p0_improvements.rationale: File/photo/mobile flows are the densest local lesson group and need more detail than the current general file and mobile guides.

p0_improvements.title: Add debug-log triage and public-safe log handling
p0_improvements.rationale: Debug logs are high value but high leak risk; the repo should teach summarization and failure-layer triage.

p0_improvements.title: Add dedicated compound address field guidance
p0_improvements.rationale: Address logic appears across Apex, LWC, metadata, validation, and mobile/map flows and deserves its own topic.

p0_improvements.title: Strengthen public-safety checks for private-derived material
p0_improvements.rationale: The extraction sources include logs, communication artifacts, generated files, and screenshots that must never be copied into public docs.

p1_improvements.title: Expand Apex trigger and async test guidance
Add dynamic record field LWC guidance
Update common failures and lesson catalog

p2_improvements.title: Add optional file and generated-document examples
Add static resource, email template, report, and dashboard public-risk checklist entries
Add mobile runtime verification notes to validation flows
Add prompt shortcuts for communication automation and destructive deployment
Add cross-links after new topic files exist

private_content_skipped_categories: Business workflow names and private operational terms
Customer, employee, user, vendor, and project names
Private metadata names for objects, fields, components, pages, flows, reports, dashboards, permission sets, and profiles
Record IDs, org-specific identifiers, deployment IDs, package IDs, runtime authentication markers, and test run IDs
Emails, phone numbers, usernames, domains, and private URLs
Credentials, tokens, client secrets, named credential values, authorization headers, certificates, and keys
Raw debug logs, stack traces with private IDs, screenshots, HTML exports, CSV/XLSX data, and generated documents
Email bodies, Chatter post bodies, SMS/chat text, PDF customer-facing copy, and file names
External source code and long copied prose from public archives
Local filesystem paths outside the audit inventory
recommended_next_prompt: Using WORKSPACE/LOCAL_REFERENCE_KNOWLEDGE_EXTRACTION.md and WORKSPACE/LOCAL_REFERENCE_KNOWLEDGE_EXTRACTION.json, create public-safe draft updates in WORKSPACE only for the P0 improvements. Do not modify SALESFORCE_KNOWLEDGE yet, do not commit, and do not push.
